_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Red Sox vs. White Sox preview: Sandoval makes debut in sweep bid
items.snark: Patrick Sandoval back from Tommy John purgatory to face the White Sox, who've scored zero runs in 11 innings, so basically batting practice.

items.headline: What will the Red Sox do with their rotation this weekend?
items.snark: Early's elbow is cooked, Suarez is day to day, and they're seriously wondering if they should call up Brayan Bello, which tells you everything.

items.headline: Willson Contreras injury overshadows Red Sox sixth straight win
items.snark: Sox win five straight then lose their best hitter to a foot contusion, because this franchise cannot have nice things.

items.headline: Jake Bennett allowed four hits in seven innings in 5-0 shutout win
items.snark: Bennett threw seven shutout innings and the offense actually showed up, which is rarer than a Fenway hot dog without ketchup.

items.headline: Contreras named to AL All-Star team as injury replacement
items.snark: Contreras makes the All-Star team while suspended pending appeal, which is the most Red Sox thing since trading Mookie for a bag of Fritos.
